steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: The expression $6x^3+42x^2$ has two terms. Both terms contain a factor of $6$, and both terms contain at least $x^2$, so the greatest common factor is $6x^2$.

steps.title: Factor each term
steps.content: Divide each term by the common factor to find what remains inside the parentheses. The first term leaves $x$, and the second term leaves $7$ because $42\div6=7$.

steps.title: Write the equivalent expression
steps.content: Putting those remaining factors inside one set of parentheses gives $$6x^3+42x^2=6x^2(x+7)$$ This distributes back to the original expression, so it is the equivalent form. The answer is C.
